channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Lula diz que vaga de vice está aberta para Alckmin, mas não o descarta no Senado
item.description: President Lula stated that the vice-presidential spot on his 2026 re-election ticket is open for Geraldo Alckmin, but the decision to run for executive or Senate will be up to Alckmin. He also announced that Minister Simone Tebet will run for the Senate from São Paulo, switching from MDB to PSB and changing her electoral domicile from Mato Grosso do Sul. Lula criticized former Central Bank President Roberto Campos Neto, linking him to the Banco Master scandal and calling for a thorough investigation.

item.title: Lula diz que Master é 'ovo da serpente' de Bolsonaro e de Roberto Campos Neto, ex-presidente do BC
item.description: President Lula stated that the Banco Master case is the 'spawn of the serpent' of the Bolsonaro government and Roberto Campos Neto's tenure at the Central Bank, claiming the bank was created in 2019 and used for frauds causing R$ 50 billion in losses. He vowed to fully investigate and denied that the PT or his government is responsible, calling it an attempt to shift blame.

item.title: Os riscos de faltar diesel e de paulada nos preços no Brasil
item.description: Brazil faces diesel shortage risks due to constrained global supply and Petrobras' below-market pricing, which discourages private imports. Although stocks may last about 25 days, experts warn that without price adjustments, scarcity could worsen, particularly as the Lula government pushes for state-level subsidies to mitigate electoral fallout.

item.title: Haddad anuncia pré-candidatura ao Governo de SP, nega barganha e diz que entra para ganhar
item.description: Fernando Haddad (PT) announced his pre-candidacy for Governor of São Paulo at an event with Lula, denying that his decision is motivated by political bargains and stating he is running to win. He emphasized the need for a clean project to awaken the electorate’s conscience in São Paulo. Lula praised Haddad’s dialogue skills with Congress and urged attendees to use their cell phones to defeat the far right.

item.title: Ao lado de Lula, Haddad é confirmado como pré-candidato ao governo de São Paulo
item.description: Fernando Haddad was confirmed as the PT's pre-candidate for governor of São Paulo in an event alongside President Lula, after announcing his departure from the Finance Ministry. Haddad rejected the notion that his candidacy is a 'sacrifice,' calling it a privilege to fight for the party. The PT aims to prevent the opposition from gaining a large advantage in São Paulo, a state where it has never won a gubernatorial election.

item.title: Defesa de Roberta Luchsinger aponta erro em relatório da PF
item.description: Roberta Luchsinger's defense told the STF that the Federal Police report contains an error in claiming she paid R$641,000 to the owner of a company linked to Fábio Luís Lula da Silva, 'Lulinha'. The defense argues the amount or transaction is incorrect, challenging the validity of the suspicions against the businesswoman and the president's son.

item.title: Haddad confirma pré-candidatura ao governo de São Paulo
item.description: Finance Minister Fernando Haddad (PT) confirmed his pre-candidacy for governor of São Paulo in the 2026 elections, following weeks of internal PT pressure and meetings with President Lula. Haddad stated he will focus on the campaign starting in April, after the party transfer window closes, and will leave the Finance Ministry on Friday, to be replaced by Dario Durigan. Environment Minister Marina Silva and Entrepreneurship Minister Márcio França are being considered for the vice-governor and Senate candidacy, respectively.

item.title: PT anuncia pré-candidatura de Haddad ao governo de SP
item.description: The PT announced Fernando Haddad as its pre-candidate for governor of São Paulo at an event in the Metalworkers' Union of ABC. Despite the announcement, a Datafolha poll shows Governor Tarcísio de Freitas leading with 44% of voting intentions against Haddad's 31% in a first-round scenario. Other candidates such as Kim Kataguiri, Paulo Serra, and Felipe D'Avila perform poorly, with 11% blank/null/none and 1% undecided.

item.title: Comitê do Senado dos EUA aprova novo indicado de Trump para Segurança Interna
item.description: The U.S. Senate Homeland Security Committee voted 8-7 to advance Markwayne Mullin’s nomination to lead the Department of Homeland Security, backed by nearly all Republicans and one Democrat, John Fetterman. Despite the committee approval, committee chair Rand Paul voted against the nomination and threatened to block it, citing concerns over Mullin’s undisclosed international travel while in the House. A final confirmation vote is expected in the Senate floor next week, amid pressure to expedite Trump’s mass deportation agenda.

item.title: Papo Antagonista – Pesquisa desaprova Erika Hilton na Comissão das Mulheres
item.description: A poll mentioned on Papo Antagonista shows disapproval of Deputy Erika Hilton as a potential president of the Women’s Commission in the Chamber of Deputies. It was also reported that Sergio Moro has joined the PL party, Jair Bolsonaro’s party, to run for governor of Paraná in 2026. The program featured Bruno Soller commenting on the poll results.

item.title: Lula destaca importância de terras raras do Brasil para América do Sul
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva defended in São Bernardo do Campo that Brazil's rare earths and critical minerals are key to restoring citizenship in South America, rejecting foreign exploitation as a form of neocolonialism. He likened external attempts to extract these resources to historical actions by the Spanish and English, asserting that only Latin American nations can solve their own sovereignty and development challenges. Lula also honored former Uruguayan president Pepe Mujica, awarding him an honorary doctorate in memoriam from UFABC, praising his humanity and leadership.

item.title: Governo Lula vê sinal positivo em encontro de Trump com líder do Japão
item.description: The Lula administration views the meeting between Donald Trump and Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi as a positive signal that the U.S. diplomatic agenda is being unlocked after the Middle East war. This raises expectations that the previously agreed-upon meeting between Lula and Trump, set for January, may soon take place, focusing on cooperation against organized crime, critical minerals, and regional security.

item.title: Guerra do Irã altera eleição brasileira e obriga Lula a se mexer
item.description: The Iran war is driving up oil and gas prices, fueling inflation and threatening Lula’s 2026 re-election bid. The government has responded by eliminating federal diesel taxes and pressuring states to cut ICMS, fearing a trucker strike like in 2018 that boosted Bolsonaro. With crude above $100 per barrel and no signs of the conflict ending soon, the energy crisis has become an unpredictable and potentially decisive factor in Brazil’s election.

item.title: Pressão por domiciliar a Bolsonaro mobiliza Tarcísio, Flávio, Michelle, deputados e ministros do STF
item.description: Allies of Jair Bolsonaro, including Flávio and Michelle Bolsonaro, Governor Tarcísio de Freitas, bolsonarist congressional deputies, and even STF ministers, are pressuring Alexandre de Moraes to grant house arrest to the former president, citing his worsened health following hospitalization for bacterial aspiration pneumonia. The strategy also invokes Moraes’ alleged fear of being implicated in investigations tied to the Banco Master case — involving his wife — and argues that Bolsonaro’s potential death in prison would be politically blamed on the minister.

item.title: Lula é aguardado em sindicato do ABC para lançamento de pré-candidatura de Haddad
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva accompanied Finance Minister Fernando Haddad to the headquarters of the ABC Metalworkers Union in São Bernardo do Campo (SP) for the launch of Haddad’s pre-candidacy for the São Paulo state governorship. The event was widely anticipated as a key moment in the PT’s political strategy for the 2026 elections, underscoring Lula’s strong backing of his ally’s campaign.

item.title: Lula prega união da América do Sul em defesa de terras raras
item.description: President Lula urged greater unity among South American nations in the protection and sustainable exploitation of Brazil's critical minerals and rare earths, during an event honoring former Uruguayan president Pepe Mujica in São Bernardo do Campo. He emphasized that these resources should support the recovery of Latin American citizenship, avoiding a repeat of colonial-era exploitation patterns. This was Lula's second engagement in São Paulo on that day.

item.title: A 1ª pesquisa testando a pré-candidatura de Vanderlei Luxemburgo ao Senado no Tocantins
item.description: The first poll testing Vanderlei Luxemburgo's pre-candidacy for the Senate in Tocantins will be released by Real Time Big Data next Wednesday (25), based on 1,600 interviews with a two-point margin of error. The survey will pit Luxemburgo (Podemos) against Alexandre Guimarães (MDB), Carlos Gaguim (União), Eduardo Gomes (PL), Irajá Abreu (PSD), Wanderley Barbosa and Amélio Cayres (Republicanos), while also updating voter intentions for the state governor race.

item.title: Secretário da Polícia Civil do Rio anuncia que deixará cargo para concorrer às eleições
item.description: Felipe Curi, Rio de Janeiro's Civil Police Secretary, announced he will resign to run for a seat in the Chamber of Deputies in the 2026 elections. He made the announcement at a Lide event in Copacabana, accompanied by Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, stating his public security experience showed the need for federal legislative changes. Curi denied allegations that the Civil Police was being used politically against councilman Salvino Oliveira.

item.title: Lula: Celac está deixando de existir porque crescimento da extrema-direita afugenta os países
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva stated that the Community of Latin American and Caribbean States (Celac) is ceasing to exist due to the growth of the far-right, which he said is 'scaring away' countries in the region. He attributed the bloc's weakening to the rise of right-wing political forces that hinder regional cooperation.

item.title: Eleitor em Dia: certificado de quitação militar para tirar o título é obrigatório apenas para os conscritos
item.description: The military discharge certificate is required only for men in the conscription class — Brazilians born between January 1 and December 31 of the year they turn 19 — to obtain a voter title. The requirement is based on the gender recorded in civil registry, regardless of gender identity. Those outside the conscription period can obtain the voter title without the certificate, even if they are already 18 years old.

item.title: Lula faz críticas a Tarcísio durante evento da Caravana Federativa em São Paulo
item.description: During the Federative Caravan event in São Paulo, President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva criticized Governor Tarcísio de Freitas for poorly receiving São Paulo mayors and for inaugurating Casa Paulista homes without acknowledging federal funding from the Minha Casa, Minha Vida program. Lula also accused Tarcísio of plagiarizing the program's name, originally created by Geraldo Alckmin during his governorship, and emphasized that mayors are always treated with dignity and respect when visiting Brasília, regardless of who is in power.

item.title: Crusoé: Tudo o que pode dar muito errado para Lula nas eleições
item.description: President Lula faces a highly volatile environment in his 2026 reelection bid, with multiple imminent risks that could trigger serious crises. The truckers' strike is highlighted as the most recent and immediate threat, capable of destabilizing the economy and generating political backlash. The article suggests these events are largely beyond Lula's control, making his reelection highly vulnerable to external and unpredictable factors.

item.title: Petistas veem Simone Tebet como peça importante para alavancar Haddad em SP
item.description: Petty officials view Simone Tebet, pre-candidate for the Senate in São Paulo, as a key asset to boost Fernando Haddad’s gubernatorial campaign due to her connections with the Centrão and center-right voters. Tebet is expected to join the PSB, transfer her voter registration, and leave the MDB to align with the PT ticket, aiming to siphon votes from Tarcísio de Freitas among moderate and business-oriented electors. Her ability to operate independently in the state’s interior is seen as a strategic advantage for expanding the campaign’s reach.

item.title: Anatel atesta que Vorcaro contatou número do STF, diz presidente da CPMI do INSS
item.description: The president of the INSS CPMI, Senator Carlos Viana, stated that an Anatel report confirms businessman Daniel Vorcaro contacted a functional number of the Supreme Federal Court. Viana sent an official letter to the STF requesting, within 48 hours, the identification of the minister who held the line during the contact, refraining from naming Alexandre de Moraes despite prior reports suggesting message exchanges between them in November 2025.

item.title: Rafael Greca se filia ao MDB para concorrer ao governo do Paraná
item.description: Former Curitiba mayor Rafael Greca left the PSD and joined the MDB to run for governor of Paraná in 2026, stating his move aims to strengthen the presidential campaign of Governor Ratinho Junior (PSD), as a single party cannot launch a presidential candidate. Greca, who serves as Secretary of Sustainable Development in Ratinho’s administration, said alliances will be built and that the people will decide the electoral outcome. His MDB affiliation will be celebrated in Brasília at a 60th-anniversary party event on March 23, 2026.

item.title: Delegados de polícia fazem gesto a Tarcísio após projeto de lei
item.description: The São Paulo Police Delegates Union (SINDPESP) praised Governor Tarcísio de Freitas for a bill altering the career progression model in the Civil Police, stating it fulfills a significant part of his 2022 campaign promises. The union noted the reform modernizes the institution and ensures objective predictability in promotions, while emphasizing that further measures, such as salary improvements, are still needed.

item.title: CPMI do INSS: presidente diz que linha do STF manteve contato com Vorcaro e pede que tribunal informe quem usa o número
item.description: The president of the INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPMI), Senator Carlos Viana (Podemos-MG), requested information from the STF regarding a phone number linked to the court that allegedly had contact with Daniel Vorcaro, owner of Banco Master. He asked the STF’s General Director to disclose who used the number over the past five years within 48 hours, following evidence of communications between Vorcaro and an STF employee from phone data. Minister Alexandre de Moraes denied receiving messages from Vorcaro, while Minister André Mendonça blocked access to the CPMI’s secure room to preserve confidentiality.

item.title: Após governo endurecer regras, caminhoneiros desistem de greve
item.description: Truckers decided against launching a strike after the federal government issued Provisional Measure No. 1.343/2026, which tightens enforcement of the minimum freight floor and enhances protections for the sector. The decision was made during an assembly at Sindicam in Santos, following an assessment of rising diesel costs and outdated freight rates, while maintaining dialogue with authorities. Despite dissatisfaction with fuel expenses, leaders of Abrava and Sindicam opted to avoid paralysis, prioritizing negotiation and price monitoring.

item.title: Ao vivo: Lula participa de homenagem a Mujica em SP
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva attends the ceremony awarding an in memoriam honorary doctorate to former Uruguayan President José "Pepe" Mujica, organized by UFABC in São Bernardo do Campo. The event honors Mujica’s legacy as a leader committed to democracy, education, social justice, and regional integration. Lula emphasized their friendship and shared political values, including Mujica’s visit during Lula’s 2018 imprisonment and attendance at his 2023 inauguration.

item.title: Deputada que fez blackface em SP declarou-se parda à Justiça Eleitoral
item.description: State deputy Fabiana Bolsonaro (PL) performed blackface in the São Paulo Legislative Assembly while criticizing federal deputy Erika Hilton (PSOL), claiming to be white despite having self-declared as parda (brown) to Electoral Justice in 2022 to receive R$1,593.33 from the Special Campaign Fund. She has no relation to Jair Bolsonaro, adopting the surname due to ideological affinity. Lawmakers are seeking her removal over racist and transphobic conduct.

item.title: Corregedor-geral nega pedido de produção antecipada de provas sobre desfile de escola de samba
item.description: The Electoral Justice's Corregedor-General, Minister Antonio Carlos Ferreira, denied the Liberal Party's (PL) request for anticipatory production of evidence regarding the Acadêmicos de Niterói samba school parade, stating there was no proof the petitioner had exhausted administrative avenues or faced unjustified refusal or omission in accessing documents. The petition sought to gather evidence for a potential Judicial Electoral Investigation Action (AIJE) concerning alleged political favoritism in the parade themed around Lula.

item.title: Novamente aliados, Moro e família Bolsonaro esquecem ruptura de 2020; relembre
item.description: Senator Sergio Moro (União Brasil) announced his affiliation with the PL to run for governor of Paraná in 2026, rekindling alliances with the Bolsonaro family after their 2020 rupture. The move, backed by Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), follows a rapprochement underway since 2022, overcoming the 2020 crisis when Moro was labeled a 'traitor' for accusing Bolsonaro of interfering in the Federal Police.

item.title: Secretário de Polícia Civil do Rio deixa cargo por vaga na Câmara
item.description: Rio de Janeiro's Civil Police Secretary Felipe Curi announced he will resign his position to run for a seat in the Chamber of Deputados in the 2026 elections. Curi stated his decision is driven by the need for legislative changes in public security, aiming to influence policy at the federal level.

item.title: Gilmar e o falso garantismo a proteger Toffoli
item.description: The article criticizes Supreme Court Minister Dias Toffoli, accusing him of involvement in corruption scandals, lacking professional qualifications, and resisting political pressure to retire, suggesting his continued presence harms Lula's government and the Supreme Court's image. Toffoli is described as a 'zero to the left' with a history of failing public exams and weak academic performance.

item.title: Flávio participa de evento do grupo empresarial de Doria e ataca Lula
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) attended a lunch hosted by Lide, the business group founded by João Doria, delivering a 20-minute speech. In his remarks, he praised the Anti-Faction Bill as a 'great watershed' and attacked President Lula, claiming there are 'high chances' he will side with criminals when deciding on the bill’s sanctions, calling Lula 'ignorant' and the PT government 'backward' and 'incompetent'.

item.title: Macri reaparece y postula a su partido como el “próximo paso” para Argentina tras Milei
item.description: Mauricio Macri reemerges in Argentine politics, positioning his PRO party as the 'next step' for the country following Javier Milei's administration. He aims to capitalize on growing corruption suspicions surrounding Milei to present PRO as an alternative offering stability and real progress. Macri emphasizes the need to connect Milei's purported economic stability with tangible improvements in living conditions for the population.

item.title: Deputada do ‘blackface’ se declarou branca em 2020 e parda em 2022
item.description: State deputy Fabiana Bolsonaro (PL-SP), who performed a blackface act against federal deputy Erika Hilton (PSOL-SP), changed her racial self-declaration from white in 2020 to brown in 2022, receiving campaign funds allocated for Black and brown candidates. Hilton filed a judicial complaint alleging ideological falsity, arguing that the fraudulent racial reclassification undermines the proportional distribution of public campaign funds and trivializes racism when followed by a blackface incident.

item.title: Saiba quem é Dario Durigan, escolhido de Lula para comandar a Fazenda
item.description: President Lula appointed Dario Carnevalli Durigan, 41, as the new Minister of Finance, replacing Fernando Haddad, who is running for governor of São Paulo. Durigan, a former Haddad aide in São Paulo city hall and a member of the Finance Ministry team since 2023, was selected for his trustworthiness and closeness to the president. A law graduate from USP and master’s holder from UnB, he previously worked at the AGU, the Civil House, and as Director of Public Policies at WhatsApp before rejoining the government.

item.title: Problemas na Justiça dificultam vida do Avante durante janela partidária
item.description: The Federal District Electoral Court denied Avante's request for an injunction, keeping the party suspended due to failure to file financial reports. This blocks the party from running candidates in the upcoming elections in the DF and complicates efforts by lawmakers to join the party during the partisan window, owing to legal uncertainty.

item.title: Em pré-campanha, Flávio Bolsonaro elogia modelo de segurança de Bukele
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), a pre-candidate for president in 2026, praised El Salvador President Nayib Bukele's security model during a pre-campaign speech. He advocated for building new prisons and reducing the age of criminal responsibility in Brazil as measures to combat crime.

item.title: Aumento das penas para furto, roubo e receptação vai para as mãos de Lula
item.description: Brazil's Congress approved increased penalties for theft, robbery, and receiving stolen goods, awaiting President Lula's sanction or veto. The reforms raise maximum sentences for theft from 4 to 6 years, up to 10 years for cyber-related crimes, and impose harsher penalties for robbery involving electronics or firearms, and for latrocínio, raising the minimum sentence from 20 to 24 years. Receiving stolen goods will now be served in closed regime, with penalties of 2 to 6 years.

item.title: Gilmar Mendes diz que o Brasil tem uma ‘dívida’ com Alexandre de Moraes
item.description: Gilmar Mendes stated that Brazil owes a 'debt' to Alexandre de Moraes, praising his leadership in the Fake News Inquiry and other cases as vital to preventing an 'authoritarian abyss'. He commended Moraes’ courage and integrity, despite recent controversies surrounding his alleged ties to Daniel Vorcaro and the Banco Master scandal, including a contract involving Moraes’ wife.

item.title: Athletico-PR vence o Cruzeiro pelo Brasileirão
item.description: Athletico-PR defeated Cruzeiro 2-1 at Arena da Baixada, with goals from Mendoza and Viveros in the first half and Villarreal pulling one back for Cruzeiro in the second. The win moved Athletico-PR to 10 points and 7th place in the 2026 Brasileirão with a 55% win rate, while Cruzeiro remains last with only 3 points from seven matches, three draws and four losses, and a goal difference of -8.

item.title: Lula critica Tarcísio em dia do anúncio de Haddad: ‘Prefeitos em SP são pouco ou mal recebidos’
item.description: President Lula criticized Governor Tarcísio de Freitas for allegedly poorly receiving São Paulo mayors and for inaugurating Casa Paulista housing units without acknowledging federal funding through Minha Casa, Minha Vida. Lula also accused Tarcísio of plagiarizing the program's name, originally created by Geraldo Alckmin, and emphasized that all mayors deserve respectful treatment in Brasília regardless of political affiliation.

item.title: Rafael Greca se filia ao MDB de olho no governo do Paraná
item.description: Rafael Greca announced his affiliation with the MDB to run for governor of Paraná in 2026, aiming to challenge Sergio Moro and support Ratinho Junior's presidential project. The move comes one day after the PL declared its support for Moro, intensifying polarization in the state race. Greca stated he is returning to the MDB because he believes democracy must come first, without polarization, with public spirit and commitment to the common good.

item.title: Gadêlha come banana ao citar Neymar e é chamado de racista
item.description: Federal Deputy Túlio Gadêlha (Rede-PE) posted an Instagram video commenting on Neymar's absence from the Brazil national team call-up and ended by eating a banana, sparking accusations of racism as the gesture is widely recognized as a racist insult toward Black players in European football stadiums. Users criticized the post, noting Neymar had previously been targeted with a banana thrown at him by Espanyol fans in 2014. Gadêlha also referenced Wagner Moura's Oscar loss, linking it to right-wing celebrations.

item.title: Flávio defende castração química para estupradores e redução da maioridade penal
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) advocated for reducing the age of criminal responsibility to 14 and implementing chemical castration for rapists during a Lide group lecture, attended by Rio de Janeiro’s Civil Police Secretary Felipe Curi. The proposals reflect a hardline stance on public security.

item.title: Biografia de fundador da Universidade Zumbi dos Palmares é lançada hoje em SP
item.description: The biography 'The Sun Shone at Night', about José Vicente, founder of Zumbi dos Palmares University, is being launched today in São Paulo. Written by journalist Ricardo Viveiros, the book includes testimonies from Lula, Alckmin, Temer, and Martinho da Vila, highlighting his fight for racial inclusion in education and employment.

item.title: Ministros destacam “coragem” de Moraes no julgamento de Bolsonaro
item.description: Supreme Court ministers Luiz Edson Fachin and Gilmar Mendes praised Minister Alexandre de Moraes for his 'courage' in handling cases related to an alleged coup plot attributed to former President Jair Bolsonaro, emphasizing his role in the January 8, 2023 attacks and his defense of democratic order. They noted that Moraes held Bolsonaro accountable under the law, affirming the STF's role as a constitutional guardian, even amid U.S. Magnitsky sanctions that were later lifted.

item.title: Gilmar virou cabo eleitoral de senadores anti-STF em 2026
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Gilmar Mendes used his authority to annul decisions from the Senate's CPI investigating organized crime, acting to protect his ally Dias Toffoli. This positions him as an electoral operative for senators opposing the STF in 2026.
